questions.stem: In evolution, what is disruptive selection?

choices.label: A
choices.text: selection favoring both extremes over the intermediate phenotype

choices.label: B
choices.text: selection favoring intermediate phenotypes over the extremes

choices.label: C
choices.text: selection that maintains multiple alleles, as with sickle-cell heterozygotes

choices.label: D
choices.text: selection favoring one extreme phenotype, shifting the average

questions.explanation: disruptive selection is selection favoring both extremes over the intermediate phenotype.

hints.prompt: Natural selection is grouped into modes based on which part of a trait's distribution it favors. Which statement about these modes is correct?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Stabilizing selection favors intermediate phenotypes and tends to reduce variation

choices.label: B
choices.text: Directional selection favors both extremes at the same time

choices.label: C
choices.text: Every mode of selection increases genetic variation

choices.label: D
choices.text: Stabilizing selection shifts the population mean toward one extreme

hints.rationale: Stabilizing selection favors intermediates, which frames how the other modes are defined.

hints.prompt: How does disruptive selection act on individuals that have the average (intermediate) trait value?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It strongly favors the intermediate phenotype

choices.label: B
choices.text: It selects against the intermediate phenotype

choices.label: C
choices.text: It has no measurable effect on any phenotype

choices.label: D
choices.text: It converts every individual to a single extreme

hints.rationale: Disruptive selection works against the middle of the distribution.

hints.prompt: One main answer choice actually describes balancing selection (heterozygote advantage), not a mode named for which part of the distribution is favored. Which choice can you eliminate on that basis?

choices.label: A
choices.text: selection that maintains multiple alleles, as with sickle-cell heterozygotes

choices.label: B
choices.text: selection favoring intermediate phenotypes over the extremes

choices.label: C
choices.text: selection favoring both extremes over the intermediate phenotype

choices.label: D
choices.text: selection favoring one extreme phenotype, shifting the average

hints.rationale: The sickle-cell heterozygote example is balancing selection, a different concept, so it can be ruled out.

questions.stem: A canned food is heated to boiling (100 C) but some bacterial contaminants survive and later cause spoilage. Which bacterial feature best explains this survival?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Formation of heat-resistant endospores

choices.label: B
choices.text: Presence of a capsule

choices.label: C
choices.text: Gram-negative outer membrane

choices.label: D
choices.text: A single polar flagellum

questions.explanation: Endospores are dormant, extremely heat-resistant structures; boiling may not kill them, which is why autoclaving (steam under pressure) is used for sterilization.

hints.prompt: Boiling water (100 C) reliably kills most vegetative bacterial cells. Why is boiling therefore not considered true sterilization?

hints.prompt: The contaminants withstood 100 C and later revived to spoil the food. Which property must the responsible bacterial structure have?

questions.stem: In genetics, what is genetic linkage?

hints.prompt: Genes located on the same chromosome may not assort independently. Which statement is correct?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Genes on the same chromosome can tend to be inherited together

choices.label: B
choices.text: All genes always assort completely independently

choices.label: C
choices.text: Genes on the same chromosome never travel together

choices.label: D
choices.text: Chromosomal location has no effect on inheritance

hints.rationale: Genes on the same chromosome can be inherited together, i.e., linked.

hints.prompt: Genetic linkage is strongest when two genes are located how, relative to each other, on a chromosome?

choices.label: A
choices.text: close together

choices.label: B
choices.text: far apart on different chromosomes

choices.label: C
choices.text: at opposite ends of the chromosome

choices.label: D
choices.text: location does not matter

hints.rationale: The closer two genes are, the more tightly linked (inherited together) they are.

hints.prompt: Which main choice describes crossing over (the exchange of segments between homologous chromosomes in prophase I), a different process, and can be eliminated?

hints.prompt: Osmosis moves water across the membrane depending on the relative solute concentration outside the cell. Which statement is correct?

choices.label: A
choices.text: If the surrounding solution is more concentrated than the cell, water leaves the cell

choices.label: B
choices.text: Water always enters the cell regardless of the surroundings

choices.label: C
choices.text: The solute concentration outside has no effect on the cell

choices.label: D
choices.text: Cells are incapable of losing water

hints.rationale: When the outside solute is higher, water leaves the cell by osmosis.

hints.prompt: The description specifies a cell LOSING water and SHRINKING. This happens when the outside solution is which of the following?

choices.label: A
choices.text: more concentrated (higher solute) than the cell

choices.label: B
choices.text: less concentrated than the cell

choices.label: C
choices.text: equal in concentration to the cell

choices.label: D
choices.text: pure water with no solute

hints.rationale: Losing water and shrinking means the outside solution is more concentrated.

hints.prompt: Which main choice describes a HYPOtonic solution (which causes a cell to GAIN water and swell), the opposite effect, and can be eliminated?

hints.rationale: A hypotonic solution causes swelling, the opposite of shrinking, so it is eliminated.
